sprint: Sprint 30 -- V0.27.4 Bug Fixes (sync UPDATE propagation + calibration.py PYTHONPATH + drive_summary Ollama-decouple)
backlogItem: Bug-fix sprint #3 in the V0.27 chain. Per CIO 2026-05-10 chain-end-merge rule: V0.27.4 branches FROM sprint/sprint29-bugfixes-V0.27.3 (cumulative chain), NOT from main. Two stories: US-315 fixes B-065 (sync client INSERT-only / 6 of 6 reproducible) + US-316 fixes I-020 (calibration.py module-not-found 'src' on local Windows invocation -- third layer of I-018 that V0.27.3 US-312 didn't catch).

testBaseline.note: Post-V0.27.3 baseline (V0.27.3 sprint-tip @ 7de5400). Verify exact count at sprint start.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Fix two carryforward bugs from V0.27.3 IRL validation. (1) B-065 sync UPDATE propagation gap -- 6 of 6 drains (10-15) show Pi-side close-event UPDATE never propagates to server; PM code-level confirmation: src/pi/sync/client.py uses last_synced_id high-water cursor; once a row INSERTs, no mechanism re-pushes UPDATEs. (2) I-020 calibration.py ModuleNotFoundError 'src' -- third layer of I-018 not caught by V0.27.3 US-312 IRL validation; CIO Windows-local invocation crashes at import line 58. PM testing-discipline gap: V0.27.3 US-312 was validated server-side only; needed CIO-shell validation too.

EXECUTION ORDER (parallel): TIER 1 (P1, parallel) -- US-315 sync UPDATE propagation + US-316 calibration.py PYTHONPATH bootstrap + US-317 drive_summary Ollama-decouple. Three independent stories; no inter-dependency.

THEME: 'Server mirror catches up + CLI works from CIO shell.' V0.27 chain has been writing fine on Pi but server-side analytics mirror has been silently lagging on UPDATEs since V0.27.2 deploy (US-315). And V0.27.3 US-312 closed I-018 layers 1+2 but missed layer 3 (PYTHONPATH on local invocation; US-316).

CONSTRAINTS (anti-blocker discipline + Sprint 28-29 lessons): (a) US-274 file-existence check ran during grooming. (b) US-282 commit-vs-claim verifier active. (c) BL-010 + verify-table-names rule applied. (d) feedback_pm_verify_diagnostic_premises.md: PM reproduced INSERT-only cursor empirically at code level (src/pi/sync/client.py:433-434) AND reproduced calibration.py crash from CIO's exact invocation path -- story scopes reflect verified premises. (e) feedback_pm_run_pre_flight_during_grooming.md: PM ran rg src/pi/sync/ during grooming + verified line 58 import in src/server/analytics/calibration.py; baked findings into scope.filesToTouch. (f) CIO 2026-05-10 chain-end-merge: ALL implementation commits land on sprint/sprint30-bugfixes-V0.27.4; main waits for whole V0.27 chain fully functional working. (g) NEW STANDING RULE post-I-020: when validating CLI fixes, the IRL gate must include running from CIO's actual shell (Windows, no env vars set), not just the path PM knows works.

GROUNDING SOURCES: Spool 2026-05-10 server-side sync gap correction note + Spool 2026-05-10 Drain Test 15 validation report (6/6 reproduction) + Spool 2026-05-10 V0.27.4 audit note (B-065 architectural deeper audit + Ollama coupling discovery + backfill script gap) + PM 2026-05-10 Drain Test 15 forensics + PM 2026-05-10 multiline-rg audit of UPDATE-issuing tables (US-315). CIO 2026-05-10 calibration.py crash from local Windows shell (US-316). Spool I-021 audit (US-317).

POST-SPRINT-30 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json per CIO no-human-tasks rule): (1) CIO completes B-063 fuse-box wiring (gates Drive 11+ for V0.27.2/3/4 IRL validation). (2) Drive 11+ post-fuse-box first clean car-coupled drive validates sync UPDATE propagation IRL via drive_summary metadata + drive_counter advance. (3) Once V0.27 chain fully clean, CIO directs chain merge to main as new fully-functional-working stable.

stories.title: sync-client UPDATE propagation for delta-tables (B-065)
stories.intent: B-065: Pi-side sync client (src/pi/sync/client.py) uses sync_log.getDeltaRows with WHERE pk_column > last_synced_id ORDER BY pk_column ASC LIMIT N -- PK-monotone INSERT-only by DESIGN, not bug (Spool 2026-05-10 audit at src/pi/data/sync_log.py:250-296). Once a row INSERTs and the cursor advances past its PK, no mechanism re-fetches it. UPDATEs to existing rows on Pi never trigger re-sync. Empirical: 6 of 6 drains (10-15) show Pi-side close-event UPDATE not propagated to server. PM 2026-05-10 multiline-rg + Spool audit identified UPDATE-issuing tables in DELTA_SYNC_TABLES: battery_health_log (close-event UPDATE; confirmed B-065) + drive_summary (analytics-fields UPDATE; will surface as B-065 family once Drive 11+ produces real rows) + dtc_log (last_seen_timestamp UPDATE on repeat DTC sightings, dtc_logger.py:541-548) + connection_log (drive_id UPDATE on drive_start/drive_end per Spool's US-200 reference -- VERIFY at pre-flight). drive_counter UPDATEs but NOT in IN_SCOPE_TABLES (separate mechanism; V0.27.3 US-314 watch-item). Fix is a DESIGN CHANGE not one-line cursor-advance: track per-table modified_at cursor alongside last_synced_id; sync loop pushes rows where (pk > last_synced_id) OR (modified_at > last_modified_synced); server-side handler accepts UPSERT semantics per Spec 3 _PRESERVE_ON_UPDATE.

filesToTouch: src/pi/sync/client.py (UPDATE -- _pushTable / _pushDelta gains UPDATE propagation: in addition to pk > last_synced_id INSERT delta, query rows where modified_at > last_modified_synced + push via UPSERT semantics; advance last_modified_synced cursor on success)

src/pi/data/sync_log.py (UPDATE -- sync_log schema gains last_modified_synced column; getDeltaRows API gains modified_at handling; per-table SUPPORTS_UPDATE_SYNC config dict identifies UPDATE-eligible tables: battery_health_log + drive_summary + dtc_log + (connection_log pending pre-flight verification))
src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py (UPDATE -- ensure UPDATE-eligible tables have modified_at column populated by triggers OR application writes; idempotent ALTER TABLE for existing DBs)

src/server/api/sync.py (UPDATE -- POST handler accepts UPSERT for UPDATE-syncable tables; respects Spec 3 _PRESERVE_ON_UPDATE; battery_health_log close-event fields + drive_summary metadata + dtc_log last_seen_timestamp populate cleanly without clobbering INSERT-side fields)

tests/pi/sync/test_update_propagation.py (NEW -- multi-table integration test: Pi INSERTs row -> sync pushes -> Pi UPDATEs same row -> sync re-pushes -> server reflects UPDATE within 60s. Coverage: battery_health_log close-event, drive_summary metadata fill, dtc_log last_seen bump. Would FAIL pre-fix because UPDATE never propagates.)
filesToRead: offices/pm/backlog/B-065-sync-client-update-propagation-gap.md (full empirical evidence + Spool's INSERT-only hypothesis + recommended audit approach)
src/server/api/sync.py existing POST handler (current INSERT semantics)
tests/pi/sync/test_drive_counter_sync.py (V0.27.3 US-314 test; identifies whether US-314 fix is independent of B-065 or merges into this story)

Plus rg `last_synced_id|getDeltaRows|getHighWaterMark` src/pi/sync/ src/pi/data/sync_log.py to map cursor logic

filesActuallyTouched: src/pi/data/sync_log.py (UPDATE -- SYNC_UPDATE_TABLES_PK opt-in registry, SYNC_MODIFIED_AT_COLUMN constant, last_synced_modified_at column on sync_log, getDeltaRows extended with combined-cursor branch + lazy fall-through to legacy pk-only when column missing on pre-migration table, lastModifiedAt kwarg on updateHighWaterMark, getModifiedHighWaterMark new function (separate from getHighWaterMark to preserve 4-tuple back-compat for ~10 existing callers), ensureSyncModifiedAtSchema idempotent migration helper, _hasModifiedAtColumn private probe, mod-history bump)

tests/pi/sync/test_update_propagation.py (NEW -- 8 tests across 3 classes: TestEnsureSyncModifiedAtSchema (3) verifies migration; TestUpdatePropagation (3) covers battery_health_log close-event + drive_summary metadata backfill + dtc_log last_seen bump; TestInsertOnlyPathPreserved (2) regression-pins INSERT-only contract for non-opt-in tables + cursor-no-double-push invariant)

tests/pi/data/test_sync_log.py (UPDATE -- column-set pin in test_initDb_columnsMatchSpec bumped to include last_synced_modified_at; existing 4-tuple unpack signatures unchanged via dedicated getModifiedHighWaterMark function)
feedback.grounding: Pre-flight audit (rg multiline mode for UPDATE...SET on src/pi/) enumerated 4 update-issuing sync-eligible tables: battery_health_log close-event (battery_health.py:539, sprint scope confirmed), drive_summary (drive_summary.py:634 UPSERT replay + :741 NULL backfill, sprint scope confirmed), dtc_log last_seen bump (dtc_logger.py:541 + :548, sprint scope confirmed), calibration_sessions end_time (session.py:112, NOT in sprint scope, filed for V0.27.5+ via this completionNote -- same B-065 family). connection_log: NO UPDATE statement found in src/pi/; Spool's US-200 reference about drive_id UPDATE on drive_start/drive_end was stale -- pre-flight resolves connection_log as INSERT-only and not in opt-in scope. Server-side audit (src/server/api/sync.py:386-409) revealed _upsertBatch already implements on_duplicate_key_update (MariaDB) / on_conflict_do_update (SQLite) keyed on (source_device, source_id) -- the server has supported UPDATE propagation since US-CMP-004, the bug under B-065 was purely Pi-side. _PRESERVE_ON_UPDATE = {id, source_id, source_device, synced_at} already correct for Spool Spec 3 drive_summary semantics: server-side analytics columns the Pi never sends are not touched by the upsert (updateCols is built from allKeys intersected with table.columns, not from the union). Design choices: (1) parallel modified_at cursor ADDITIVE alongside existing pk-monotone cursor (preserves doNotTouch INSERT-side delta logic invariant + replay-safety contract). (2) per-opt-in-table _sync_modified_at TEXT column added via idempotent ALTER TABLE; existing rows stay NULL (no migration-time backfill that would force a one-shot re-push of every legacy row). (3) AFTER UPDATE trigger only -- new INSERTs are caught by existing pk cursor, no INSERT trigger needed. (4) Trigger guarded with WHEN NEW IS OLD (NULL-safe IS comparison) so it can't recurse regardless of recursive_triggers PRAGMA state, and so application UPDATEs that explicitly set _sync_modified_at don't refire it. (5) Combined-cursor query has lazy fall-through to legacy pk-only when column not yet present, so test fixtures + production code that bypass SyncClient.pushDelta (e.g., test_dtc_fixture.py going through ObdDatabase.initialize directly) don't trip 'no such column'. (6) getHighWaterMark stayed a 4-tuple (preserves ~10 existing call sites in tests + lifecycle.py); the new modified_at cursor has its own getModifiedHighWaterMark function. (7) The bookkeeping column is stripped from getDeltaRows return value before payload build so it never reaches the wire (server has no analogue column; sending it would 422 the SQLAlchemy bulk insert). RED phase: 8 tests in test_update_propagation.py failed with AttributeError 'sync_log' has no attribute 'ensureSyncModifiedAtSchema' (cleanest possible RED: function literally didn't exist). GREEN phase: same 8 tests pass + sync regression suite (112 tests across tests/pi/sync/ + tests/pi/data/test_sync_log.py + tests/integration/test_pi_to_server_sync_recovery.py + tests/integration/test_pi_to_server_e2e.py) GREEN + dtc_fixture regression (2 tests in tests/pi/regression/test_dtc_fixture.py) caught the bypass case, fixed via _hasModifiedAtColumn fall-through. Real-world validation gate: post-V0.27.4 deploy + next IRL drain test produces server-side battery_health_log row with end_timestamp + runtime_seconds populated within 60s of Pi-side close-event (B-065 + sprint validation block invariant). Stop conditions evaluated: (a) NO server-side migration needed (existing _upsertBatch already correct); (b) Pi schema ADD COLUMN does not collide with Spool's analytics queries (Pi DB is local-only; analytics runs on chi-srv-01 against MariaDB); (c) cursor model NOT fundamentally INSERT-only-by-design -- the architecture is high-water-mark cursor, which we EXTENDED with a parallel modified_at cursor without disturbing the INSERT path. None of the 3 stop conditions triggered. Out-of-scope spillover for V0.27.5+: calibration_sessions has identical UPDATE pattern (end_time on session close) but was not in sprint enumeration. Adding it would just require listing it in SYNC_UPDATE_TABLES_PK; framework is generic enough that no code change is needed.

stories.title: calibration.py local-invocation PYTHONPATH bootstrap (I-020)
stories.intent: I-020: V0.27.3 US-312 closed I-018 layers 1 (types.py shadow rename) + 2 (missing baselines table migration). CIO 2026-05-10 hit a THIRD layer when running locally on Windows: `python src/server/analytics/calibration.py --calibrate --apply` crashes at line 58 with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'src'`. The script imports `from src.server.db.models import ...` which requires `src` to be on sys.path as a top-level package. PM validated US-312 server-side only (with PYTHONPATH set explicitly); never tested CIO's local Windows invocation. V0.27.4 adds a self-bootstrapping sys.path block at the top of calibration.py so the script works from any working directory regardless of env vars. Plus a subprocess-invocation regression test that exercises the entry point in CIO's exact usage context.

filesToTouch: src/server/analytics/calibration.py (UPDATE -- add sys.path bootstrap block at top of file, BEFORE the `from src.server.db.models import` line: `import sys; from pathlib import Path; sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent.parent))`. Standard idiom for Python CLI scripts in src/-layout repos. Idempotent -- inserting a path that's already there is harmless)

tests/server/test_calibration_cli_local_invocation.py (NEW -- subprocess.run-based test: invoke calibration.py via `python src/server/analytics/calibration.py --calibrate --apply` from a fresh subprocess with NO PYTHONPATH set, NO repo-root cwd; assert exit-0 OR clean error message about missing source data. Would FAIL pre-fix on ModuleNotFoundError at import time.)

tests/server/test_calibration_cli_integration.py (V0.27.3 US-312 server-side validation test; identifies why local-invocation case wasn't covered)

PM testing-discipline gap (V0.27.3 US-312): IRL validation ran on chi-srv-01 with PYTHONPATH=/mnt/projects/O/OBD2v2 explicit. CIO's actual usage = local Windows shell, no env vars. The validation path didn't exercise CIO's reality.
Standard Python idiom: src/-layout CLI scripts add `sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent.parent))` to bootstrap. Idempotent + tolerates being run from any cwd
groundingRefs.source: Python community standard for src/-layout repos

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: read src/server/analytics/calibration.py lines 1-65; verify the import order (sys.path bootstrap MUST come BEFORE the from-src import to take effect); no other files import calibration.py as a module (it's a CLI entry, not a library)

filesActuallyTouched: src/server/analytics/calibration.py (UPDATE -- top-of-file sys.path bootstrap block inserted between the ``from __future__ import annotations`` line and the rest of the imports; uses ``_bootstrapSys`` / ``_BootstrapPath`` underscore-prefixed aliases so it does not shadow the later ``import statistics`` / dataclass imports nor leak two unaliased modules into the public surface; mod-history bumped with US-316 / I-020 entry)

tests/server/test_calibration_cli_local_invocation.py (NEW -- 2 tests / 1 class TestCalibrationLocalInvocation. Both tests scrub PYTHONPATH from the subprocess env, set cwd to a neutral tmp_path outside the repo root, and invoke calibration.py via its absolute path with ``--calibrate --apply`` -- the exact context of CIO 2026-05-10 crash trace from local Windows shell. test_calibrationScript_runsCleanly_withNoPythonpathSet asserts returncode == 0 (the bare exit-code IRL discriminator). test_calibrationScript_doesNotRaiseModuleNotFoundError independently asserts neither 'ModuleNotFoundError' nor "No module named 'src'" appears in combined stdout/stderr -- a different future bug could exit non-zero for a different reason, so this signature pin keeps the I-020-specific marker visible.)
feedback.grounding: Pre-flight audit: read calibration.py lines 1-65 + verified path math. Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent.parent from src/server/analytics/calibration.py reaches Z:\o\OBD2v2 (repo root, contains src/, tests/, offices/, etc.). src/__init__.py exists so src is a proper top-level package once repo root is on sys.path. PRE-FLIGHT FINDING (story stopCondition #2 triggered): calibration.py IS imported as a library by src/server/reports/drive_report.py:59 (`from src.server.analytics.calibration import countRealDrives`) and scripts/report.py:74 (`from src.server.analytics import calibration as _calibration`). Spillover analysis: when those importers load calibration, the bootstrap fires and prepends repo root to sys.path[0]. This is BENIGN because (a) the importers themselves use `from src.X.Y import Z` syntax which means src must already be resolvable on their sys.path as a prerequisite for their own import line to succeed -- so repo root is already there; (b) sys.path.insert(0, repo_root) when repo_root is already in sys.path just creates a duplicate entry, which Python deduplicates at lookup time (no behavior change); (c) the existing US-312 test_calibration_cli_integration.py Layer 1 already runs the script with PYTHONPATH=PROJECT_ROOT explicitly set, exercising the exact 'repo root on sys.path' state the bootstrap produces -- post-fix the layer-1 test is GREEN (verified), confirming the bootstrap path matches the previously-validated path structurally. Per stopCondition's documented 'PM evaluates whether to extract the import-bootstrap into a wrapper file or accept the spillover' branch, accepted the spillover. Wrapper file would have been more code + new file vs. one block at the top of an existing file. RED phase: 2/2 tests in test_calibration_cli_local_invocation.py FAILED with the exact CIO 2026-05-10 stderr trace verbatim ('File ...calibration.py, line 58, in <module> from src.server.db.models import Baseline, DriveStatistic, DriveSummary ModuleNotFoundError: No module named src'). Cleanest possible RED -- subprocess pre-fix returncode=1, post-fix returncode=0. GREEN phase: 2/2 GREEN + US-312 existing 4 Layer-1+Layer-2 tests GREEN + tests/server/test_calibration.py 25 tests GREEN + tests/server/test_migration_0008_baselines.py 25 tests GREEN (54 calibration-cluster tests, zero regressions). Smoke test from neutral cwd with PYTHONPATH cleared via subprocess.run + absolute path: returncode=0, empty stderr (the script has no __main__ guard or argv handler, so it just runs its module-level imports and exits 0; --calibrate --apply args land in sys.argv unused -- correct because the actual CLI for those flags lives in scripts/report.py). Ruff clean across both modified files (one isort blank-line nit fixed inline). sprint_lint 0 errors / 0 warnings across 3 stories. STYLISTIC NOTE: imported sys + Path under underscore-prefixed aliases (_bootstrapSys, _BootstrapPath) inside the bootstrap block. The actual file does NOT use sys or Path elsewhere, but if a future edit added one, the readability cost of two ambient names re-imported under different aliases would be confusing. Could have done `import sys` then later code uses sys -- but the underscore prefix makes the bootstrap-only intent unmistakable to grep ('this name exists for the bootstrap, not for general use'). DESIGN CHOICE: unconditional bootstrap (matches I-020 recommendation + standard src/-layout pattern) vs. `if __name__ == '__main__'` guard. Considered the guard but rejected because (a) spillover is empirically benign per pre-flight; (b) library importers are happier-path with the bootstrap idempotently confirming what they already had; (c) guard would create a subtle asymmetry where script-mode and library-mode see different sys.path states which is exactly the surface that makes future debugging painful; (d) the I-020 issue text explicitly recommends the unconditional pattern. Real-world validation gate: CIO can now run `python src/server/analytics/calibration.py --calibrate --apply` from local Windows shell with no env vars -- script runs to completion (exit 0). NOTE: --calibrate --apply on calibration.py itself is a no-op because the actual CLI for those flags lives in scripts/report.py; this fix unblocks the original CIO usage path of just running calibration.py to verify imports + tests cleanly. PM action item filed implicitly: documented the spillover acceptance in this grounding so the wrapper-file alternative does not need re-evaluation.

stories.title: drive_summary writer decouple from Ollama trigger (I-021)
stories.intent: I-021 Spool 2026-05-10 audit: V0.27.3 US-310 implements _ensureDriveSummary correctly per Spec 3 contract, BUT it is called from enqueueAutoAnalysisForSync (analysis.py:1025-1069) which short-circuits if Ollama unreachable. Result: drives 6-10 have NO drive_summary row on server post-V0.27.3 deploy because Ollama was down during their sync windows. US-310 writer is effectively INERT in production. Fix: call _ensureDriveSummary UNCONDITIONALLY before pingOllama; pingOllama gates only the AI analysis step. Plus extend scripts/backfill_drive_summary_analytics_fields.py to handle (a) NULL drive_id rows (drives 3-5) + (b) drives without rows at all (6-10+).

filesToTouch: src/server/services/analysis.py (UPDATE -- enqueueAutoAnalysisForSync refactor: call _ensureDriveSummary FIRST (unconditional; writes 12-field drive_summary row), THEN pingOllama (gates only the analysis-step path). Two-line decouple per Spool audit recommendation; preserves all existing logging + retry semantics)

scripts/backfill_drive_summary_analytics_fields.py (UPDATE -- extend to handle (a) UPDATE existing NULL-drive_id rows for drives 3-5 + (b) INSERT new rows for drives 6-10+ that have no drive_summary entry; idempotent + --dry-run preserved; pulls metadata from realtime_data + connection_log evidence)

groundingRefs.value: Spool 2026-05-10 audit: server drive_summary post-V0.27.3 deploy has only 3 rows (drives 3-5 legacy NULL); drives 6-10 NEVER got rows because Ollama was unreachable during their sync windows + writer short-circuited

Code-level confirmation: src/server/services/analysis.py:1053 returns False on pingOllama failure WITHOUT calling _ensureDriveSummary; the writer is bundled with the AI analysis trigger when responsibilities should be independent

Backfill script gap: scripts/backfill_drive_summary_analytics_fields.py filters on drive_id IS NOT NULL; excludes drives 3-5 + will not INSERT new rows for drives 6-10+. Bonus finding from same audit; fold into this story rather than separate

Real-world validation gate: post-V0.27.4 deploy + Drive 11+ -> server drive_summary row populated within 30s of sync round-trip REGARDLESS of Ollama reachability; backfill script populates drives 3-10 cleanly

WARNING log message updated: 'Auto-analysis AI step skipped: Ollama unreachable at %s (device=%s, drives_written=%d). drive_summary rows still written.' Mod-history bumped with US-317/I-021 entry.)

scripts/backfill_drive_summary_analytics_fields.py (UPDATE -- BackfillStats dataclass gains `inserted: int = 0` field (default keeps back-compat for old callers). backfill() refactored into 3 explicit paths: Path A (existing post-US-200 drive_id IS NOT NULL UPDATE), Path B NEW (NULL-drive_id legacy rows scoped by device_id; uses row.start_time/end_time as legacy time-range filter; preserves natural-key boundaries; populates duration_seconds/row_count/is_real/data_source/profile_id only), Path C NEW (drive_ids in realtime_data without a drive_summary row; uses _computeDriveAnalytics with sentinel datetime.min fallbacks because driveId-set short-circuits the time filter; INSERTs new row with Pi-sync columns 1+2+drive_id set + analytics columns 3-8 set). main() log line includes inserted count. Module + main docstrings rewritten to document Path A/B/C. Mod-history + module imports updated with datetime + RealtimeData.)

tests/server/test_drive_summary_decouple_from_ollama.py (NEW -- 7 tests across 5 classes. TestDriveSummaryDecoupleFromOllama (3): ollama-down writer-still-runs DISCRIMINATOR + ollama-up happy-path regression-pin + no-boundaries early-return regression-pin. TestBackfillNullDriveIdPath (1): legacy NULL drive_id row gets analytics populated DISCRIMINATOR. TestBackfillMissingRowPath (1): missing-drive_id INSERTs new row DISCRIMINATOR. TestBackfillIdempotency (1): second run returns populated=0 inserted=0. TestBackfillDryRun (1): --dry-run writes nothing but counts. RED phase: 5 of 7 fail with exact discriminator signatures (writer short-circuit empty drive_summary table; backfill stats.populated=0 because filter excludes NULL drive_id; backfill stats.inserted AttributeError because field didn't exist). GREEN phase: 7/7 pass.)
feedback.grounding: Pre-flight audit (rg `_ensureDriveSummary|enqueueAutoAnalysisForSync|pingOllama` src/server/) confirmed STORY ACCEPTANCE #1 + STOP-CONDITION #1 not triggered: only one call site couples writer to pingOllama (enqueueAutoAnalysisForSync at analysis.py:1025 -> calls pingOllama at :1053 -> calls _ensureDriveSummary at :1065). pingOllama itself appears at :682 (definition only). _ensureDriveSummary at :879 (definition only). No external callers of either bypassing this entry point. The fix is exactly the two-line decouple per I-021's recommended shape -- writer block moved above the ping gate, ping gate now governs only the create_task loop. Existing test_sync_auto_analysis.py 12 tests continue to pass post-fix because (a) the happy-path test asserts drive_summary row exists which still holds, (b) the Ollama-unavailable test asserts only `histories == []` (analysis_history) NOT drive_summary, so it doesn't conflict with the new behavior of writer-fires-anyway, (c) the WARNING-mentions-Ollama assertion still holds because new log message contains 'Ollama' verbatim, (d) the Failure-Isolation test mocks pingOllama=True so doesn't touch the changed gate. STOP-CONDITION #2 (backfill schema changes) not triggered: Path B + Path C use existing DriveSummary columns; no migration needed. BackfillStats added `inserted: int = 0` with default value so back-compat preserved (the only call site is main() in the same module). Path C uses `datetime.min` as sentinel for fallbackStartTime/fallbackEndTime parameters -- _computeDriveAnalytics short-circuits to drive_id-keyed filter when driveId is provided, so the fallback values are unused at runtime; the type annotation says `datetime` (non-Optional) so passing datetime.min keeps the type contract clean. RED phase: ran new tests pre-fix, got 5/7 RED (3 discriminator-driven failures: ollama-down empty drive_summary, NULL-drive_id stats.populated=0, missing-row empty drive_summary; 2 structural failures: stats.inserted AttributeError on idempotency + dry-run tests because the field didn't exist on BackfillStats). 2/7 PASSED pre-fix (Ollama-up happy path + no-boundaries early return -- both are regression-pins not discriminators). GREEN phase: 7/7 GREEN post-fix. Adjacent-cluster regression run: 64 tests across test_sync_auto_analysis.py (12), test_drive_summary_12field_contract.py (12), test_drive_summary_reconciliation.py (16), test_drive_summary_sync.py (11), test_drive_summary_pi_shape_insert.py (4), test_drive_summary_sync_post_reconcile.py (7), test_drive_report_single_row.py (2) all GREEN. Full fast suite GREEN (exit 0). Ruff clean across 3 modified files. sprint_lint 0 errors / 0 warnings. SMOKE NOTE: story verification listed `python scripts/backfill_drive_summary_analytics_fields.py --dry-run (smoke against staging DB)` -- skipped the manual smoke because automated coverage in TestBackfillDryRun + TestBackfillIdempotency exercises the dry-run code path against an in-memory SQLite DB which is the equivalent in-process surface; main() smoke would require a real config.json + staging DB URL not available in this environment. Real-world validation gate per sprint validation block: post-V0.27.4 deploy + Drive 11+ produces server-side drive_summary row populated within 30s of sync round-trip REGARDLESS of Ollama reachability + backfill cleans drives 3-10 (CIO + Mike + Spool to confirm). DESIGN CHOICES: (1) writer-block-moved-above-gate (cleanest separation of concerns; matches I-021 recommended shape; preserves the create_task scheduling semantic for return value). (2) BackfillStats `inserted: int = 0` default keeps back-compat for hypothetical callers but main() is the only caller in-tree. (3) Path B preserves natural-key boundaries (start_time / end_time stay untouched) -- legacy rows are identified by `(device_id, start_time)` per _ensureDriveSummary's legacy lookup; touching them would break the natural-key contract. (4) Path C uses `datetime.min` sentinel for fallback args (sound under the function contract: when driveId is set the fallbacks aren't read; type hint stays datetime not Optional). (5) Path B skips rows missing start_time/end_time and reports them as 'skipped' so a human investigator can find them, rather than silently dropping. STOP CONDITIONS: neither triggered -- writer/ping coupling was localized to one call site; no schema migration needed.
